ranked_movies.title: The Yellow Sea
ranked_movies.reason: The central focus of the most upvoted post. Heavily praised for its brutal action, fantastic score, and gripping, twisted story. Described as an underappreciated masterpiece.

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended multiple times as an underappreciated Korean film. Described as wild, insane, and a movie best experienced blind.

ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a masterpiece and the crowning achievement of director Bong Joon-ho. A benchmark in the discussion comparing Korean directors.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as potentially the best film in Bong Joon-ho's filmography and a key reason to choose his work over Park Chan-wook's.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Named as one of the best films ever seen by a commenter and a cornerstone of Park Chan-wook's highly praised filmography.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Alongside 'Decision to Leave', it was argued to be superior to 'Parasite' and 'Memories of Murder' by some, highlighting Park Chan-wook's versatility.

ranked_movies.title: Decision to Leave
ranked_movies.reason: Paired with 'The Handmaiden' as a reason to favor Park Chan-wook. Praised for its aesthetics and tactile filmmaking style.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as the previous, arguably better film by the director of 'The Yellow Sea'. Noted for being harder to watch but highly effective.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon during the discussion on great Korean filmmakers.

ranked_movies.title: A Dirty Carnival
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as another example of an underappreciated Korean movie that deserves more love.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among Bong Joon-ho's top-notch films, contributing to the debate on his filmography's quality.

ranked_movies.title: Peppermint Candy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an older, impactful Korean film that has stayed with viewers long after the ending.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, the Bad, the Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended alongside 'I Saw the Devil' as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon.

ranked_movies.title: Sea Fog
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as another underappreciated Korean film in the same thread discussing 'The Yellow Sea'.

ranked_movies.title: Asura: The City of Madness
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as another example of a Korean movie that deserves more recognition.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to Dongmakgol
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a potential candidate for an underappreciated Korean film, though the commenter was unsure of its general reception.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Indirectly mentioned as the inspiration for a 'Star Trek' episode title ('Shuttle to Kenfori'), confirming its cultural impact and recognition.

ranked_movies.title: Decibel
ranked_movies.reason: A more recent film (2022) that a commenter mentioned liking after seeing it in the theatre.

ranked_movies.title: Bugonia
ranked_movies.reason: The upcoming Yorgos Lanthimos remake of 'Save the Green Planet!' was a central topic of box office forecasting, generating significant discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Mickey 17
ranked_movies.reason: Bong Joon-ho's film was heavily discussed and criticized in the director debate, with many considering it a major misstep after 'Parasite'.
